vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible. P3 (Pothos) is present, identified by the white rabbit anchor.

P3.observations: The Pothos specimen remains anchored by the white rabbit figurine. No significant foliage growth or decline observed over the 5-day period.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'dormant observation'. Only P3 shows definitive presence. The lack of visible foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests either extreme youth (seedling stage) or total loss.
anomalies: Presence of a plant tag in T-1 image which vanished in the current frame.
Persistent low-light conditions hindering full botanical assessment.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a sequential audit of the provided images. The environment is highly controlled but suffers from low-light visibility issues. P3 is the only confirmed occupant. The yellow pot (P1) showed a marker in the middle sequence, suggesting an attempt at identification or recent planting, but it is currently obscured. The overall health of the visible Pothos is stable, though the biome is significantly under-populated relative to the registry.
